questions.prompt: Your JSON parser breaks 5% of the time because Claude wraps responses in markdown fences. What's the canonical Claude fix?

choices.text: Strip markdown with regex after the fact.

Prefill the assistant message with `{` so Claude continues from there. Markdown fences and 'Sure! Here's...' preamble disappear because there's no room for them. Combine with few-shot for total reliability.

questions.explanation: Prefilling is Claude's built-in feature for locking response format. By adding `{ role: 'assistant', content: '{' }` as the last message, you start Claude's response mid-document. Parse failures drop to zero, no post-processing needed.

questions.prompt: You want to extract ONLY a French translation, with no commentary. What pattern works best?

choices.text: Prefill `<french>` and set `stop_sequences: ['</french>']`. Claude generates only the translation, stopping before any wrap-up commentary. Bonus: latency drops because there's less to generate.
Increase max_tokens to give room for commentary then strip it.
Trust the model to be terse.

questions.explanation: Prefilling + stop sequences is the standard Claude pattern for extracting a single field. The opening tag locks where the response starts; the stop sequence ensures Claude doesn't keep going. Clean, fast, deterministic.
